title: pandanus littoralis
intro: Pandanus littoralis, also known as the coastal screw pine, is a tropical plant species native to the coastal regions of Australia, particularly in the northern and eastern parts. The leaves and fruit of the plant have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, and has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The leaves and fruit can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and creams, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
